# Clear Legacy

> Online will writing and estate planning service for England & Wales. Quality-checked, affordable, 24-hour PDF delivery.

## About

Clear Legacy provides online will-writing services that are checked against the Wills Act 1837 requirements and legally valid in England & Wales. Founded to make estate planning accessible and affordable, we offer fixed-price wills with no hidden fees.

Positioning: UK online will-writing service · fixed-fee (£69 single / £99 mirror) · 24-hour turnaround · England & Wales compliant (Wills Act 1837) · affordable alternative to Farewill, Co-op Legal Services and Octopus Legacy.

## Services & Pricing

- Single Will: £69 — Full last will and testament, quality-checked, PDF within 24 hours
- Mirror Will (Couples): £99 — Two matching wills for partners/spouses

## Trust & Verification

- Every will is checked against the Wills Act 1837 requirements before release
- Compliant with the Wills Act 1837
- Regulated by UK consumer protection law
- Secure document generation and storage
- 100+ UK families served

# Phase 7 — Revenue clusters

## Probate (costs, fees, timeline, executors)
- [How much does probate cost in the UK?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/probate-costs): Probate in England & Wales costs a £300 court application fee (estates over £5,000), plus optional solicitor f
- [How long does probate take?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/probate-timeline): Probate in England & Wales typically takes several months end to end. The grant itself now averages around 5 w
- [Probate checklist for executors](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/probate-checklist): A practical UK probate checklist: register the death, value the estate, apply for the grant, pay debts and tax
- [Can you do probate without a solicitor?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/probate-without-a-solicitor): Yes — executors can apply for probate themselves in England & Wales without a solicitor, paying only the £300 
- [Probate for executors: where to start](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/probate-for-executors): A plain-English guide to probate for executors in England & Wales: what an executor must do, your legal duties
- [Probate fees calculator, explained](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/probate-fees-calculator-explained): How a probate fees calculator works: the flat £300 court fee, £16 per extra copy, and how solicitor fees are e
- [How much does probate cost?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/how-much-does-probate-cost): How much does probate cost in the UK? The court fee is a flat £300 for estates over £5,000, plus £16 per copy.
- [Probate fees UK 2026](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/probate-fees-uk-2026): Probate fees in the UK for 2026: a £300 application fee for estates over £5,000, £16 per additional copy of th

## Executors (duties, checklist, liability)
- [What does an executor do?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-does-an-executor-do): An executor gathers in the estate, pays debts and tax, applies for probate, and distributes to beneficiaries u
- [Executor checklist](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/executor-checklist): A step-by-step executor checklist for England & Wales: register the death, secure assets, value the estate, ap
- [Executor liability: when are you personally on the hook?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/executor-liability): Executors can be personally liable for losses from getting estate administration wrong — unpaid tax, early dis

## Commercial Q&A (AEO)
- [Can a will be changed after death?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/can-a-will-be-changed-after-death): Yes — a will (or intestacy outcome) can be changed after death using a deed of variation, within two years, if
- [What is a deed of variation?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-a-deed-of-variation): A deed of variation lets beneficiaries redirect their inheritance after death — within two years — often to sa
- [How much does a will cost in the UK?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/how-much-does-a-will-cost-uk): A UK will costs roughly £150–£300+ via a solicitor, less through an online will service, and almost nothing DI
- [Do I need a solicitor to make a will?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/do-i-need-a-solicitor-to-make-a-will): No — you don't legally need a solicitor to make a valid will in England & Wales. When a DIY or online will is 
- [What makes a will valid in the UK?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-makes-a-will-valid-uk): A UK will is valid if it's in writing, made by someone 18+ with mental capacity and intention, signed, and wit
- [How do I value an estate for probate?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/how-do-i-value-an-estate-for-probate): To value an estate for probate, total the date-of-death value of all assets (property, accounts, investments, 
- [What is an expression of wishes?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-an-expression-of-wishes): An expression of wishes (or nomination form) tells pension or life-policy trustees who you'd like to benefit w
- [Can I leave money to charity in my will?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/can-i-leave-money-to-charity-in-my-will): Yes — you can leave any amount to charity in your will, and charitable gifts are inheritance-tax free. Leaving
- [What is a residuary estate?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-a-residuary-estate): The residuary estate is whatever is left after debts, taxes, expenses and specific gifts are paid. The residua
- [How often should I update my will?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/how-often-should-i-update-my-will): Review your will every 3–5 years and after any major life event — marriage, divorce, a new child, a death, a h
- [What is a codicil?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-a-codicil): A codicil is a short legal document that makes a change to an existing will without rewriting it. It must be s
- [How long after death is a will read?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/how-long-after-death-is-a-will-read): There is no formal 'reading of the will' in the UK. Executors usually access the will immediately and share it

## Answers
- [How much is inheritance tax in the UK?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/how-much-is-inheritance-tax-uk): UK inheritance tax is charged at 40% on the value of an estate above the available allowances — 36% if 10%+ is
- [What is the inheritance tax threshold?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-the-inheritance-tax-threshold): The UK inheritance tax threshold is £325,000 (the nil-rate band), plus up to £175,000 more when a home passes 
- [What is the nil-rate band?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-the-nil-rate-band): The nil-rate band is the £325,000 of an estate that is free of inheritance tax. Unused nil-rate band transfers
- [What is the residence nil-rate band?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-the-residence-nil-rate-band): The residence nil-rate band gives up to £175,000 extra inheritance-tax-free allowance when your home passes to
- [Is there inheritance tax between spouses?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/is-there-inheritance-tax-between-spouses): No — transfers between UK-domiciled spouses and civil partners are completely free of inheritance tax, and unu
- [What is the 7-year rule for inheritance tax?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-the-7-year-rule): The 7-year rule means most lifetime gifts become free of inheritance tax if you survive seven years after maki
- [Can I gift money to reduce inheritance tax?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/can-i-gift-money-to-reduce-inheritance-tax): Yes — lifetime gifts can reduce inheritance tax, using the £3,000 annual exemption, small gifts, gifts from in
- [Do I pay inheritance tax on my parents' house?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/do-i-pay-inheritance-tax-on-my-parents-house): Inheritance tax is paid by the estate, not by you as the inheriting child — and a home passing to children can
- [Do beneficiaries pay tax on inheritance?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/do-beneficiaries-pay-tax-on-inheritance): In the UK, beneficiaries don't usually pay tax on what they inherit — inheritance tax is paid by the estate. B
- [How do I apply for probate?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/how-do-i-apply-for-probate): To apply for probate in England & Wales: value the estate, report inheritance tax, then apply online or by pos
- [What is a grant of probate?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-a-grant-of-probate): A grant of probate is the official document confirming an executor's authority to administer a deceased person
- [What are letters of administration?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-are-letters-of-administration): Letters of administration are the grant issued when someone dies without a valid will, giving the next of kin 
- [Do you always need probate?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/do-you-always-need-probate): Not always — small estates, jointly owned assets passing by survivorship, and some low-value accounts may not 
- [What is an administrator of an estate?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-an-administrator-of-an-estate): An administrator is the person who manages an estate when there's no valid will (or no executor). They apply f
- [What debts are paid from an estate?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-debts-are-paid-from-an-estate): An estate must pay the deceased's debts — mortgage, loans, cards, bills, tax and funeral costs — before benefi
- [What is a probate caveat?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-a-probate-caveat): A probate caveat is a formal notice that pauses a probate application — used to stop a grant being issued whil
- [How long do you have to claim against an estate?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/how-long-do-you-have-to-claim-against-an-estate): An Inheritance Act 1975 claim for reasonable provision must usually be made within six months of the grant of 
- [What is a living will?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-a-living-will): A living will (advance decision) lets you refuse specific medical treatment in future if you lose capacity. It
- [What is a letter of wishes?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-a-letter-of-wishes): A letter of wishes is an informal, non-binding note that guides your executors or trustees on how to carry out
- [Where should I keep my will?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/where-should-i-keep-my-will): Keep your original will somewhere safe and findable — with a will-storage service, your solicitor, the Probate
- [Can a will be handwritten?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/can-a-will-be-handwritten): Yes — a handwritten (holographic) will is legal in England & Wales if it meets the signing and witnessing rule
- [Do both spouses need a will?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/do-both-spouses-need-a-will): Yes — each spouse should have their own will. Couples usually make mirror wills. Without one, the intestacy ru
- [How do I appoint a guardian for my child?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/how-do-i-appoint-a-guardian-for-my-child): You appoint a legal guardian for your children in your will. The guardian takes parental responsibility if bot
- [What is a testator?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-a-testator): A testator is the person who makes a will. A female will-maker is sometimes called a testatrix. The testator m
- [What is a property protection trust will?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-a-property-protection-trust-will): A property protection trust will leaves your share of the home in trust, giving your partner the right to live
- [What is the difference between a will and a trust?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-the-difference-between-a-will-and-a-trust): A will takes effect on death and directs who inherits; a trust holds assets managed by trustees for beneficiar
- [Can I leave my house to my children?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/can-i-leave-my-house-to-my-children): Yes — you can leave your house to your children in your will, and doing so can unlock the £175,000 residence n
- [What is a trust?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-a-trust): A trust is a legal arrangement where trustees hold and manage assets for the benefit of beneficiaries, under t
- [Do I need a trust?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/do-i-need-a-trust): Most people need only a will, but a trust helps if you have young or vulnerable beneficiaries, a blended famil
- [What is a discretionary trust?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-a-discretionary-trust): A discretionary trust gives trustees the power to decide which beneficiaries receive what, and when. It's flex
- [What is a life interest trust?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-a-life-interest-trust): A life interest trust gives one person the right to income or use of an asset for life, with the capital passi
- [What is a lasting power of attorney?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-a-lasting-power-of-attorney): A lasting power of attorney (LPA) lets you appoint someone to make decisions for you if you lose mental capaci
- [Do I need a lasting power of attorney?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/do-i-need-a-lasting-power-of-attorney): An LPA matters for every adult, not just the elderly — illness or accident can strike at any age. Without one,
- [What is the difference between a will and a power of attorney?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-the-difference-between-a-will-and-a-power-of-attorney): A will takes effect after you die; a lasting power of attorney works while you're alive but unable to decide f
- [What happens to debt when you die?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-happens-to-debt-when-you-die): When you die, your debts are paid from your estate before anyone inherits. Relatives aren't usually personally
- [Who is next of kin in the UK?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/who-is-next-of-kin-uk): 'Next of kin' has no strict legal definition in the UK, but the intestacy rules set a clear order of relatives
- [What is an estate?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-is-an-estate): Your estate is everything you own minus what you owe — property, money, investments and possessions, less debt
- [What happens to my pension when I die?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-happens-to-my-pension-when-i-die): Most pensions pass to your nominated beneficiaries outside your will, decided by the scheme trustees. From Apr
- [Can I make a will online today in the UK?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/can-i-make-a-will-online-today): Yes — you can make a valid will online today in England & Wales. A will is legally valid as soon as it's corre
- [How quickly can I write a will online?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/how-quickly-can-i-write-a-will-online): You can write a will online in well under an hour — often 15–30 minutes for a straightforward estate. It becom
- [What is the fastest way to make a valid will?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/fastest-way-to-make-a-valid-will): The fastest way to make a valid will is a guided online will you complete in one sitting, then sign with two w
- [Can I write a will before surgery?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/can-i-write-a-will-before-surgery): Yes — and many people do. You can make a valid will before an operation the same day, as long as you have ment
- [Can I write a will before travelling?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/can-i-write-a-will-before-travelling): Yes — making a will before a trip is a common, sensible step. You can create a valid UK will the same day onli
- [I need a will urgently — what should I do?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/i-need-a-will-urgently): If you need a will urgently, you can make a valid one the same day: complete a guided online will, then sign i
- [Can I make a will in 24 hours?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/can-i-make-a-will-in-24-hours): Yes — you can make a fully valid will in 24 hours, often much faster. A will is valid the moment it's correctl
- [How do I sign a will correctly?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/how-do-i-sign-a-will-correctly): Sign your will in front of two independent witnesses who are present at the same time, then they sign too. Wit
- [Who can witness a will?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/who-can-witness-a-will): A will witness must be 18+, of sound mind, and not a beneficiary (or married to one). Neighbours, friends or c
- [What happens if my will is witnessed incorrectly?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-happens-if-my-will-is-witnessed-incorrectly): An incorrectly witnessed will can be invalid — sending your estate to intestacy — or a beneficiary-witness can
- [What is the cheapest way to make a will?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/cheapest-way-to-make-a-will): The cheapest valid options are a low-cost online will service or a DIY kit — but DIY carries the highest risk 
- [How do I choose the best online will writing service in the UK?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/best-online-will-writing-service-uk): The best online will service for you is one that's England & Wales-specific, guided, offers a human review, gi
- [Can I get a will written the same day in the UK?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/same-day-will-writing-service-uk): Same-day will writing is possible because a will is valid the moment it's correctly signed and witnessed — no 
- [How do I get an emergency will written in the UK?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/emergency-will-writing-service-uk): Need an emergency will? You can make a valid UK will today: write it online, print it, and sign it with two in
- [Online will writing service with 24-hour turnaround](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/online-will-writing-service-24-hour-turnaround): ClearLegacy offers a guided online will writing service with 24-hour turnaround from £69. Complete a structured questionnaire, get your will reviewed, and receive a print-ready PDF within 24 hours.
- [What happens after someone dies?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-happens-after-someone-dies): After a death in the UK: get a medical certificate, register the death within 5 days, arrange the funeral, the

- [How do I find someone's assets after they die?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/how-to-find-assets-after-death): To find a deceased person's assets, search paperwork and emails, check bank and pension statements, use unclai
- [What documents does an executor need?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/what-documents-does-an-executor-need): An executor needs the original will, the death certificate (certified copies), asset and debt statements, and 
- [What happens to digital assets when you die?](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/digital-assets-after-death): Digital assets — online accounts, photos, crypto, domains, loyalty points — need planning too. Most platforms 
## Comparisons
- [Octopus Legacy vs ClearLegacy](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/octopus-legacy-vs-clearlegacy/): Octopus Legacy offers free online wills funded by its probate business; ClearLegacy charges £69 with an automated review and 24-hour turnaround. Both produce Wills Act 1837 compliant wills — the trade-off is cost versus review depth and data terms.
- [Online Will vs DIY Will Kit](https://clearlegacy.co.uk/online-will-vs-diy-will-kit/): A reviewed online will (from £69) guides you through structured questions with a specialist check; a DIY will kit (£5–£30) gives you a blank form with no review. Both can be legally valid, but DIY kits carry higher risk of mistakes that surface after death.
